metadata.name: Billbi Native Design Manifest
metadata.designIntent: Translate the React and web hi-fi proposal into native SwiftUI decisions for a calm, dense, professional freelance invoicing app. Preserve the proposal's information hierarchy and dark, focused working surface, but use native Apple structure, controls, materials, accessibility, and platform conventions instead of recreating web chrome.
metadata.priorityStatement: macOS is the primary product surface and must become usable end to end before iOS or iPadOS polish consumes meaningful implementation time.

sourceTokenIntent.appearance: Support dark and light appearances, with dark as the proposal's primary verification target.
sourceTokenIntent.type: Use native system typography by default. Use monospaced digits for money, durations, dates, invoice numbers, and table numeric columns.
sourceTokenIntent.density: Keep desktop rows compact and scannable. Keep phone rows touch-friendly while retaining numeric clarity.
sourceTokenIntent.color: Preserve semantic accents for ready, finalized, sent, overdue, paid, danger, success, and primary actions, but route final colors through native semantic roles and accessibility contrast checks.

macOS.navigation: Use NavigationSplitView with a native sidebar for workspace areas and projects. Use a second column for project buckets where the selected destination benefits from a three-pane workflow.
macOS.windowing: Use the real macOS window and toolbar. Do not recreate HFWindow or fake traffic-light-less web chrome. The app content should fill the window with native titlebar and toolbar placement.
macOS.layout: Favor dense tables, lists, inspectors, and split views. Use fixed minimum column widths for sidebar and bucket list, with the detail pane taking remaining width.
macOS.controls: Use Button, Menu, Picker, Toggle, TextField, DatePicker, Table, Form, sheet, popover, inspector, toolbar items, and keyboard shortcuts rather than custom clickable divs.
macOS.workflow: Optimize for keyboard-first capture, quick command/search, inline row creation, and low-modal friction.
macOS.liquidGlass: Use glass only for app chrome, toolbar-adjacent controls, floating command/search affordances, transient sheets, and selected navigation surfaces where it improves hierarchy without hurting legibility.

iOS.priority: companion after Mac loop is usable
iOS.navigation: Use NavigationStack with large titles for top-level companion screens and compact navigation bars for pushed details.
iOS.layout: Use List, ScrollView, sections, safe areas, native sheets, and touch targets. Do not force desktop three-pane density onto iPhone.
iOS.controls: Use native add sheets, DatePicker or time input controls, Toggle for billable state, confirmation actions, and bottom toolbars where appropriate.
iOS.workflow: Focus on triage, quick logging, project and bucket review, ready-to-invoice confirmation, invoice viewing, and basic invoice status actions.
iOS.liquidGlass: Use glass sparingly for bottom sheets, floating add/action buttons, and navigation bars. Keep entry rows, totals, and PDF previews plain.

iPadOS.navigation: Adapt iPhone NavigationStack flows into NavigationSplitView when horizontal size class is regular, with Projects or Today in the sidebar and bucket or invoice detail beside it.
iPadOS.layout: Use larger touch-friendly rows than Mac but allow side-by-side review of bucket lists and details.
iPadOS.controls: Prefer native split views, sheets, popovers, keyboard shortcuts, and drag-safe layout. Reuse Mac information architecture where it fits, not the web screenshots.
iPadOS.liquidGlass: Mirror iOS glass choices for chrome and transient controls; keep business data and document surfaces legible.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationSplitView sidebar plus main content. Main pane uses a toolbar title, segmented Picker for active versus archived projects, LazyVGrid or Table-adjacent card rows depending window width, and recent activity below.
primaryControls: Sidebar selection for Dashboard, Projects, Invoices, Clients, Settings

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Primary Projects landing hierarchy, sidebar, toolbar actions, project totals, ready and overdue attention states.
Projects landing with lower recent activity content visible.

knownDeviations: Replace card-heavy grid with native adaptive grid or table/list behavior when the window is narrow.
Use SF Symbols instead of geometric text glyphs.
Use the native window toolbar instead of an in-content web top bar where possible.

screens.nativeStructure: Three-pane NavigationSplitView state with selected project, selected bucket, and an empty entries section in the detail pane.
primaryControls: Keyboard shortcut to begin entry capture
knownDeviations: Use a native ContentUnavailableView-style empty state or a small inline prompt instead of sketch placeholder styling.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationSplitView with sidebar, bucket list column, and detail pane. Detail pane uses header totals plus a native Table for entries, with plain rows for billable and non-billable states.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Three-pane bucket detail, table density, billable and non-billable rows, totals, mark-ready affordance.

knownDeviations: Use native Table columns on macOS instead of CSS grid.
Keep table and row backgrounds plain, not glass.
Use disclosure or inspector patterns for deeper row editing if inline editing becomes crowded.

screens.nativeStructure: Editable draft row embedded in the entries Table or directly below the table header. Use focus state, keyboard shortcuts, validation, and save-add-another behavior.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Active draft row, focus ring, calculated duration and amount, keyboard helper line.

knownDeviations: Use native focus rings and validation affordances instead of custom accent borders.
Keep helper text minimal or place it in a status area so it does not compete with the table.

screens.nativeStructure: Small inline edit or popover from the bucket header rate label, with confirmation that unlocked entries re-total while finalized invoice snapshots remain unchanged.
knownDeviations: Avoid hand-drawn annotation. Use concise native help text or a confirmation sheet when rate changes affect existing unlocked entries.

screens.nativeStructure: A plain summary section above or below the entries table showing ready status, billable hours, fixed costs, total, and Create Invoice action.
knownDeviations: Keep the ready summary as a solid, high-contrast surface rather than translucent glass.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationSplitView main dashboard with KPI summary, needs-attention table/list, and revenue chart. Use Charts for revenue history when available.
primaryControls: New invoice or quick action menu

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Dashboard KPIs, needs-attention actions, revenue chart, sidebar selection, new-invoice action.

knownDeviations: Use native Chart instead of SVG sparkline.
Use list/table rows for needs-attention actions instead of web card rows if it improves keyboard navigation.

screens.nativeStructure: Dashboard needs-attention row navigates to the bucket detail with ready summary visible and selected bucket highlighted.
knownDeviations: Do not create a separate ready-bucket screen if the normal bucket detail can express the state clearly.

screens.nativeStructure: Native modal sheet from Dashboard or BucketDetail. Content uses Form-like fields for recipient, invoice number, issue date, due date, currency, note, and a plain totals summary.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Confirm sheet proportions, fields, totals, modal backdrop, finalize actions.

knownDeviations: Use native sheet sizing and accessibility rather than absolute positioned modal.
Keep the totals strip plain for legibility, not glass.
Use native DatePicker and Picker even if their visual shape differs from HFField.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationSplitView invoice destination with metadata inspector column and PDF preview document surface. Toolbar exposes share/open, export PDF, mark sent, and later mark paid.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Invoice detail metadata column, status, activity, white PDF preview, export/open actions.

knownDeviations: Render the PDF preview as a real generated document preview if possible, not a SwiftUI recreation.
Keep PDF paper white and opaque in all appearances.
Use platform sharing/export affordances instead of custom web buttons where appropriate.

screens.nativeStructure: Sidebar destination with a searchable Table or List of clients and a detail form or inspector for billing address, email, payment terms, and archive state.
knownDeviations: No hi-fi screenshot exists; derive visual treatment from Projects and Settings surfaces.

screens.nativeStructure: Native macOS Settings scene where possible. Sections cover business profile, invoice numbering, payment details, default currency, and default tax/VAT note.
primaryControls: Invoice prefix and next number fields
knownDeviations: Use native Settings layout instead of embedding settings as another web-style content card.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationStack root or tab destination with large title and List rows for projects.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Phone projects list, large title, status pills, touch row hierarchy.

knownDeviations: Use native NavigationStack large title instead of manual top padding for Dynamic Island.
Rows should support dynamic type and standard swipe/context actions where useful.

screens.nativeStructure: Pushed NavigationStack destination with project subtitle and List rows for buckets.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Phone buckets list, back navigation, ready/finalized status display.

knownDeviations: Use native back button and toolbar item placement.
Avoid custom chevron text; use NavigationLink row affordance.

screens.nativeStructure: Pushed NavigationStack destination with compact top total, mark-ready action, sectioned entries by date, and floating or toolbar add action.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Phone bucket detail total, date grouping, row density, floating add action.

knownDeviations: Prefer toolbar add button if floating button conflicts with scroll content or accessibility.
Use native section headers, not uppercase CSS-styled divs.

screens.nativeStructure: Pushed detail screen for an entry with date, time range, duration, billable state, title/note, edit, and delete actions.
knownDeviations: Use a native Form or grouped List layout instead of sketch-only typography.

screens.nativeStructure: Bottom sheet using native presentation detents, with fields for date, from time, to time, description, computed total, billable toggle, cancel, and save.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Phone bottom sheet, focused time field, computed amount, dimmed backdrop.

knownDeviations: Use native sheet drag indicator and detents.
Use Toggle for non-billable/billable state rather than a custom pill button if clearer.

screens.nativeStructure: Bucket detail state with ready summary at top, editable entries list below, and Create Invoice action.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: Ready state summary, create-invoice action, editable entries list.

knownDeviations: Use a solid success-tinted surface with accessible contrast; do not make totals translucent.

screens.nativeStructure: iPhone bottom sheet version of the Mac confirmation form, using vertical fields and a full-width primary finalize action.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: iPhone invoice confirmation sheet and primary finalize action.

screens.implementationPriority: iOS P3 after Mac invoice flow
knownDeviations: Use native DatePicker presentation if direct text date editing is error-prone on phone.

screens.nativeStructure: Pushed invoice detail with status, amount, metadata summary, PDF preview, and bottom action bar or toolbar actions.

screenshotVerificationTargets.verifies: iPhone invoice metadata, PDF preview, and status actions.

screens.implementationPriority: iOS P3 after Mac invoice flow
knownDeviations: Use Quick Look or native document preview where practical instead of a bitmap-like SwiftUI approximation.
Keep the PDF preview white and opaque.

screens.nativeStructure: NavigationStack root or tab destination focused on companion triage: outstanding amount, overdue amount, ready buckets, month summary, needs-attention rows, and quick-log affordance.
knownDeviations: No current screenshot in inventory; verify after iOS companion work begins.
Prefer native list/section styling over dashboard card overload on small screens.

screens.nativeStructure: Bottom sheet from Today with bucket picker, recent bucket chips or menu, time fields, computed amount, and save-add-another action.
knownDeviations: Use Picker/Menu for bucket selection when chips become too many.

screens.nativeStructure: Invoice detail state for overdue invoices with prominent amount, line snapshots, note, and status actions.
knownDeviations: Reminder/email automation can be deferred; keep a Mail handoff or disabled future affordance only if the app can support it honestly.

HFWindow.webRole: Fake app window with rounded border, custom surface, no traffic lights.
HFWindow.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Use the real app WindowGroup content and native macOS window/titlebar. Configure toolbar and content background; do not build custom window chrome.
HFWindow.platformNotes: On iOS, this maps to the normal screen safe-area container. On iPadOS, it maps to the app scene and split view.

HFSidebar.webRole: Narrow workspace and project navigation column.
HFSidebar.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: NavigationSplitView sidebar containing a List with sections for Workspace and Projects. Use Label with SF Symbols and badges/counts.
HFSidebar.platformNotes: On iPhone this becomes top-level NavigationStack roots or tabs, not a persistent sidebar. On iPad regular width it becomes a native sidebar.

HFNavLabel.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: List Section headers or small secondary Text using system section styling.
HFNavLabel.platformNotes: Avoid manual uppercase where native Section header style already communicates grouping.

HFNavItem.webRole: Clickable sidebar row with active state, icon/dot, optional count.
HFNavItem.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: NavigationLink row with Label, Badge, selected state from NavigationSplitView selection, and optional colored project dot.
HFNavItem.platformNotes: Use SF Symbols for workspace areas and small color circles for project identity.

HFPill.webRole: Small status indicator with dot, tint, and label.
HFPill.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Reusable StatusBadge SwiftUI view using Text plus small Circle, semantic colors, accessibility label, and fixed compact padding.
HFPill.platformNotes: Do not use glass. Keep solid tinted backgrounds for ready, finalized, sent, overdue, paid, accent, and neutral states.

HFButton.webRole: Custom web button variants: primary, ghost, danger, sizes, icon.
HFButton.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Native Button with appropriate buttonStyle: borderedProminent for primary, bordered/plain for secondary, destructive role for danger, toolbar placement for chrome actions.
HFButton.platformNotes: Use SF Symbols in labels. Preserve keyboard shortcuts for Mac actions such as search, add, save, cancel, and mark ready.

HFCard.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Use GroupBox, Form sections, List/Table containers, or a small local bordered Surface view only when a native container is not suitable.
HFCard.platformNotes: Avoid nested cards. Keep dense business data plain and high contrast.

HFNum_HFMoney_fmtMoney.webRole: Monospaced/tabular numeric text and Swiss-style currency formatting.
HFNum_HFMoney_fmtMoney.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Text with monospacedDigit(), numeric text style, and a shared currency formatter or FormatStyle. Preserve stable width for money, durations, dates, invoice numbers, and table numbers.
HFNum_HFMoney_fmtMoney.platformNotes: Use locale-aware formatting where appropriate, but the first product pass may preserve EUR display and invoice-specific formatting from settings.

HFField.webRole: Static-looking input block with label, prefix/suffix, focus ring, and optional monospace.
HFField.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: TextField, TextEditor, SecureField if ever needed, DatePicker, Picker, or custom field row only when native controls cannot express the required compact layout.
HFField.platformNotes: Use native focus rings and validation. For time ranges, consider a focused parser field on Mac and DatePicker/time controls on iOS.

HFSectionLabel.webRole: Uppercase section heading with optional action.
HFSectionLabel.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Section header, Table header, or small secondary Text with trailing toolbar/action only when the section needs an inline command.
HFSectionLabel.platformNotes: Use native capitalization where it reads better with Dynamic Type.

HFDivider.platformNotes: Let List/Table separators handle row boundaries unless a custom layout needs explicit division.

HFTopBar.webRole: In-content top bar with title, breadcrumbs, and right actions.
HFTopBar.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Native toolbar and navigation title. Use ToolbarItem placement, NavigationSplitView title, breadcrumbs in content only when they aid orientation.
HFTopBar.platformNotes: On Mac, prefer actual window toolbar. On iOS, use navigationBarTitleDisplayMode and toolbar items.

PhoneCanvas.webRole: Manual iPhone content surface with hard-coded Dynamic Island and home indicator padding.
PhoneCanvas.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: NavigationStack or screen root respecting safeAreaInset and native system bars.
PhoneCanvas.platformNotes: Do not hard-code device padding in app code.

PhoneLargeTitle.webRole: Manual large title and optional back/right action.
PhoneLargeTitle.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: navigationTitle with large display mode plus toolbar items.
PhoneLargeTitle.platformNotes: Use native back button behavior, Dynamic Type, and swipe-back gesture.

PhoneNav.webRole: Manual centered title nav row with back/right slots.
PhoneNav.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: NavigationStack toolbar with inline title display mode.

PhoneFAB.nativeSwiftUIReplacement: Toolbar add button, bottom safe-area action, or floating Button with glass/tint only when it does not obscure scroll content.
PhoneFAB.platformNotes: On iOS, verify with Dynamic Type and scrolling so the action never covers critical rows.

candidates.element: macOS window toolbar and command/search affordance
candidates.rationale: Chrome-level controls benefit from system material separation and can feel native without reducing data legibility.
A subtle glass or material treatment can distinguish the active workspace while keeping the sidebar native.
Transient modal chrome can use material to float over the dashboard. Form fields and totals inside should remain opaque.

candidates.element: iOS bottom sheets for entry and invoice confirmation
candidates.rationale: Native sheet material and drag indicator match platform expectations. Editable controls and totals need solid backgrounds.
A small floating action may use glass or prominent tint if it remains readable and does not cover business data.

candidates.element: popover or command palette for search
candidates.rationale: Transient search surfaces are chrome, not dense data, and can use glass to communicate temporary focus.

nonGlass.element: dense entry tables and row lists
nonGlass.rationale: Rows contain dates, time ranges, descriptions, durations, and amounts that must remain crisp and stable.

nonGlass.element: invoice PDF preview and rendered PDF paper
nonGlass.rationale: Invoices are documents. Preview must represent export output faithfully and remain legible in dark and light app appearances.

nonGlass.element: editable form rows and text fields
nonGlass.rationale: Translucency makes focus, validation, and typed values harder to read, especially on Mac where forms are dense.
Totals are high-value financial data and should not sit on variable backgrounds.
Status should be instantly scannable and accessible. Glass would reduce semantic color clarity.
Revenue trends need clear axes, marks, and contrast; use native Charts styling instead of decorative translucency.

nonGlass.element: long invoice, client, settings, and profile forms
nonGlass.rationale: Long text and repeated labels need predictable contrast and native editing behavior.

nativeReplacements.flatWebCards: Prefer native List, Table, Form, GroupBox, Grid, and section containers. Use a small local Surface view only for KPI cards and summary panels where no native container fits.
nativeReplacements.sidebars: Use NavigationSplitView with List sections, NavigationLink selection, Label, Badge, and SF Symbols.
nativeReplacements.sheets: Use SwiftUI sheet. On macOS, prefer centered or document-modal sheets sized by content. On iOS, use presentationDetents and native drag indicator.
nativeReplacements.buttons: Use Button with roles, keyboard shortcuts, toolbar placement, Menu, and confirmation dialogs for destructive or state-changing actions.
nativeReplacements.fields: Use TextField, TextEditor, DatePicker, Picker, Toggle, and FocusState. Custom field wrappers should be narrow and reusable only after repeated need appears.
nativeReplacements.tables: Use Table on macOS for entries, invoices, clients, and possibly projects. Use sectioned List/LazyVStack rows on iOS.
nativeReplacements.pdfPreviewSurfaces: Use real PDF generation output shown through PDFKit/QuickLook or a native document preview surface. Always keep preview paper white and opaque.
nativeReplacements.statusPills: Use StatusBadge with semantic tint, small dot, text, accessibility label, and no glass.
nativeReplacements.charts: Use Swift Charts for revenue history and dashboard trends. Keep chart marks subtle and readable.
nativeReplacements.commandSearchAffordance: Use .searchable, Commands, keyboard shortcut Cmd+K, or a command palette popover/sheet. Search should route to projects, buckets, invoices, and clients.
nativeReplacements.icons: Replace proposal glyphs with SF Symbols: dashboard, folder/project, doc.text, person.2, gearshape, plus, magnifyingglass, square.and.arrow.up, arrow.down.doc, checkmark, clock, exclamationmark.triangle.
nativeReplacements.moneyAndDates: Centralize formatting for currency, durations, dates, invoice numbers, and monospaced digits. Avoid scattering string formatting in views.

macOSFirst.goal: After each Mac increment, build and launch the real macOS app, capture screenshots, and compare native output against the manifest and current Mac proposal screenshots.

verify: Glass only on allowed sheet chrome if used

iOSLater.goal: Only after the Mac loop is usable, build and run on a booted iOS simulator, capture screenshots, and compare companion flows against the iPhone targets.

deviations.decision: Use real macOS window and toolbar instead of HFWindow.
deviations.why: Native windowing, resizing, titlebar behavior, accessibility, and toolbar commands matter more than matching fake web chrome.

deviations.decision: Use NavigationSplitView and NavigationStack rather than custom flex columns and manual back buttons.
deviations.why: Native navigation gives platform gestures, selection behavior, keyboard support, restoration, and iPad adaptation.

deviations.decision: Reduce card-heavy web layout where native lists/tables/forms express the data better.
deviations.why: The app is operational software. Dense repeated data should scan like native business UI, not like a marketing dashboard.

deviations.decision: Use system fonts and monospaced digits rather than web fonts Geist and Fira Code.
deviations.why: System typography handles Dynamic Type, platform metrics, and accessibility. Monospaced digits preserve numeric stability.

deviations.decision: Replace decorative glyph icons with SF Symbols and accessible labels.
deviations.why: Symbols are localizable, consistent, and better supported by Apple platforms.

deviations.decision: Use native controls for dates, currency, toggles, text, menus, and pickers.
deviations.why: Native controls provide validation, keyboard/focus behavior, accessibility, and platform input affordances.

deviations.decision: Apply glass to chrome and transient containers only, not financial data surfaces.
deviations.why: Legibility and trust are more important than visual novelty for invoices, entries, totals, and forms.

deviations.decision: Show the actual generated PDF or a faithful native PDF preview, always on opaque white paper.
deviations.why: Users must inspect the document they will send. The preview should not inherit app theme glass or dark surfaces.

deviations.decision: Allow toolbar or bottom safe-area alternatives to the screenshot FAB.
deviations.why: A floating button can obscure content at large Dynamic Type or small screens; native placement should win if needed.

deviations.decision: Treat sketch storyboards as behavioral states, not visual style targets.
deviations.why: They capture flows like rate change, empty state, and entry detail, while hi-fi files and screenshots define the stronger visual language.

implementationTaskHints.title: Establish native app shell
implementationTaskHints.scope: NavigationSplitView sidebar, workspace destinations, toolbar/search placeholder, project selection state, native window behavior.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Mac app launches with real window chrome and sidebar; screenshot compared to Projects and Dashboard targets.

implementationTaskHints.title: Model and render core dashboard/project data
implementationTaskHints.scope: Dashboard KPIs, project list, bucket counts, status badges, recent activity using seed or persistent data.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Focused tests for totals/status derivation plus Mac screenshot of Projects and Dashboard.

implementationTaskHints.title: Build bucket detail and entry table
implementationTaskHints.scope: Project bucket list, bucket header, time entry table, fixed cost support, non-billable display, totals.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Tests for billable/non-billable totals and screenshot against Bucket Detail.

implementationTaskHints.title: Add keyboard-first inline entry capture
implementationTaskHints.scope: Draft row, time parser, focus movement, save, save-and-add-another, cancel, telemetry for create entry.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Tests for duration parsing and entry creation; simulator/app run with logs proving save path.

implementationTaskHints.title: Implement ready bucket state
implementationTaskHints.scope: Mark-ready action, ready summary, edit path, create-invoice seed data.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Tests for bucket status transitions and screenshot of ready state once visible.

implementationTaskHints.title: Create invoice confirmation flow
implementationTaskHints.scope: Confirmation sheet, invoice number, recipient snapshot, dates, currency, note, totals, finalize action, telemetry.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Tests for invoice snapshot finalization and screenshot against confirm sheet.

implementationTaskHints.title: Generate and preview PDF invoice
implementationTaskHints.scope: Simple replaceable invoice template, PDF render/open/export, invoice detail view, activity events.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Tests for PDF output data and screenshot against finalized PDF target; logs prove render/open/export actions.

implementationTaskHints.title: Add clients and settings needed for real invoicing
implementationTaskHints.scope: Client CRUD, business profile, invoice numbering, currency, payment details, tax/VAT note.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Focused persistence/domain tests and manual Mac app run through client/profile editing.

implementationTaskHints.title: Polish Mac native structure and Liquid Glass decisions
implementationTaskHints.scope: Audit chrome versus data surfaces, apply glass only to approved candidates, ensure tables/forms/PDF stay plain.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Mac screenshots explicitly checked against glass and non-glass decisions.

implementationTaskHints.title: Begin iOS companion projects/buckets/bucket detail
implementationTaskHints.scope: NavigationStack Projects, Buckets, Bucket Detail, Add Entry sheet, Ready Bucket state.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Only after Mac is usable: build/run on booted iOS simulator and compare screenshots to iOS targets.

implementationTaskHints.title: Add iOS invoice confirmation and invoice detail
implementationTaskHints.scope: Phone invoice confirmation sheet, finalized invoice detail, PDF preview, PDF/share/status actions.
implementationTaskHints.verification: iOS screenshots compared to invoice confirm/detail targets.

implementationTaskHints.title: Add Today and quick-add companion flows
implementationTaskHints.scope: Today dashboard triage, quick add any bucket, overdue invoice action path.
implementationTaskHints.verification: New screenshots captured because current inventory lacks Today, Quick Add, and overdue action targets.

implementationTaskHints.title: Adapt iPad layouts
implementationTaskHints.scope: Regular-width split view adaptation for Projects/Buckets/Bucket Detail and invoice review.
implementationTaskHints.verification: Run on iPad simulator after iOS work begins; verify split layout does not simply stretch iPhone cards.

assumptionsAndLimitations: This manifest is a design translation artifact only and intentionally does not modify app source, tests, UI tests, or Xcode project files.

Current screenshot inventory contains Mac hi-fi targets and several iPhone targets, but does not include screenshots for Today, Quick Add, Entry Detail, rate-change, empty bucket, Clients, or Settings.

The iPhone screenshot inventory includes invoice confirmation and invoice detail states that are not exported as named HiFi_Phone functions in hifi-iphone.jsx; they are still included because they are present in the provided screenshots and match the Mac invoice flow.
The manifest names SwiftUI destinations as implementation guidance. Later agents should adapt exact type names to the existing app structure.
No Liquid Glass code decision here should override legibility, accessibility, or platform availability checks during implementation.
